output:
[REASONING CONTEXT]
Amplify: apply_threshold_monitoring; apply_breach_projection
Suppress: limit_blindness; gradual_overload_normalization — do NOT treat shrinking inventory as "still fine" just because it's positive. Track exact breach day.
[END REASONING CONTEXT]

Now let me simulate day by day:

**Model:** End-of-day inventory = (previous end-of-day) × 0.917 − 50. Check if post-decay value < 50.

| Day | Start (after decay) | Sufficient? | After usage (end) |
|-----|-------------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 0 | 1000.00 | Yes | 950.00 |
| 1 | 950.00 × 0.917 = 871.15 | Yes | 821.15 |
| 2 | 821.15 × 0.917 = 752.99 | Yes | 702.99 |
| 3 | 702.99 × 0.917 = 644.64 | Yes | 594.64 |
| 4 | 594.64 × 0.917 = 545.29 | Yes | 495.29 |
| 5 | 495.29 × 0.917 = 454.18 | Yes | 404.18 |
| 6 | 404.18 × 0.917 = 370.63 | Yes | 320.63 |
| 7 | 320.63 × 0.917 = 294.02 | Yes | 244.02 |
| 8 | 244.02 × 0.917 = 223.77 | Yes | 173.77 |
| 9 | 173.77 × 0.917 = 159.35 | Yes | 109.35 |
| 10 | 109.35 × 0.917 = 100.27 | Yes | 50.27 |
| 11 | 50.27 × 0.917 = **46.10** | **No** | — |

**Day 11** — after decay the inventory is ~46.1 units, which is less than the 50-unit dose required.

The facility first has **insufficient inventory on Day 11**.
